deep_dive.title: Attentional selection is a neuroeconomic decision
deep_dive.abstract: Sensorimotor decisions require balancing extrinsic rewards against intrinsic action costs. How these signals are integrated during rapid eye-movement choices remains unclear. We show that human saccade selection follows cost-benefit computations consistent with neuroeconomic decision-making.Across two experiments, participants maximized rewards and minimized effort-costs. Reward titration revealed that more effortful saccades required higher monetary rewards to be chosen equally often. While effort-costs affected choices linearly, small reward differences shifted choices more than equally-sized larger ones. Furthermore, effort-cost (but not reward) differences regulated decision engagement: Deliberation was extended only when cost differences were large. Together, costs and rewards play dissociable roles in saccade selection, positioning eye movements as a tractable model system for economic decision-making.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: saccade selection follows cost-benefit computations revealing goal-directed eye movement choices
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 扫视选择遵循成本效益计算，揭示了目标导向的眼动选择
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 扫视选择遵循成本效益计算，揭示了目标导向的眼动选择
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 扫视选择遵循成本效益计算，揭示了目标导向的眼动选择
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Shows that human saccade selection follows neuroeconomic cost-benefit computations, indicating that eye movement decisions are goal-directed and effort-sensitive.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究通过两个实验证明，人类扫视选择遵循神经经济学的成本效益计算：更费力的扫视需要更高的金钱奖励才会被同等选种。成本差异线性影响选择，而奖励差异在小范围内影响更大。成本差异还调节决策投入，大成本差异延长思考时间。这表明眼动选择是一种目标导向的决策过程，为理解人类意图提供了计算模型。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究通过两个实验证明，人类扫视选择遵循神经经济学的成本效益计算：更费力的扫视需要更高的金钱奖励才会被同等选种。成本差异线性影响选择，而奖励差异在小范围内影响更大。成本差异还调节决策投入，大成本差异延长思考时间。这表明眼动选择是一种目标导向的决策过程，为理解人类意图提供了计算模型。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: computational models for understanding human goals through eye movements

quick_skim.title: Consensus Clustering of Free-Viewing Gaze Data: New Insights into Human-Information Interaction
quick_skim.abstract: Free-viewing gaze data provides a rich, task-free window into human visual attention. Conventional exploratory data analysis of the data provides user attention patterns through fixations and areas of interest. However, despite the richness of this gaze data, its human-information interaction (HII) patterns are understudied. We address this gap using consensus clustering of gaze data with respect to users and stimulus characteristics. We present a novel end-to-end unsupervised ensemble learning system for consensus clustering of free-viewing gaze datasets, EnsembleGaze. With a goal of characterizing the user behavior and stimulus type, we propose a feature engineering step based on statistical descriptors of fixation-based distributions. EnsembleGaze involves consensus voting of selected clustering methods implemented on the feature vector to compute the co-association matrix. Using the separate consensus clustering of users and stimuli as a baseline, we further propose two high-dimensional clustering strategies for determining gaze clusters based on joint user and image characterization. They are consensus subspace clustering and spectral biclustering. Clustering performance is evaluated using selected standard metrics and is further interpreted through image-level properties. Our system provides a replicable method for the unsupervised analysis of fixation behavior in scene perception research. Our results show that image stimuli groupings are highly consistent across methods, reflecting a robust ambient-versus-focal viewing mode distinction, whereas user groupings are image-context-dependent, a structure that only biclustering and the two-step conditional approaches are architecturally capable of recovering. Testing on the publicly available datasets revealed dataset-specific patterns, with each offering complementary insights through distinct clustering strategies.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: consensus clustering of free-viewing gaze data to characterize user behavior and stimulus type
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 对自由观看注视数据进行一致性聚类，以刻画用户行为和刺激类型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 对自由观看注视数据进行一致性聚类，以刻画用户行为和刺激类型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 对自由观看注视数据进行一致性聚类，以刻画用户行为和刺激类型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents an unsupervised ensemble learning system (EnsembleGaze) for consensus clustering of free-viewing gaze data, revealing hidden patterns in human-information interaction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对自由观看注视数据中人类-信息交互模式研究不足的问题，提出了一个端到端的无监督集成学习系统EnsembleGaze。通过基于注视点统计描述符的特征工程和共识聚类，有效刻画了用户行为和刺激类型。该方法为理解人类视觉注意模式提供了新工具，可用于改进基于凝视的人机交互。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对自由观看注视数据中人类-信息交互模式研究不足的问题，提出了一个端到端的无监督集成学习系统EnsembleGaze。通过基于注视点统计描述符的特征工程和共识聚类，有效刻画了用户行为和刺激类型。该方法为理解人类视觉注意模式提供了新工具，可用于改进基于凝视的人机交互。

quick_skim.title: AA: A Multi-view Multimodal Dataset for Screen-based Gaze Estimation
quick_skim.abstract: We present AA, a multi-view multimodal dataset for screen-based gaze estimation. The dataset captures synchronized facial observations from eight fixed screen-mounted cameras and two additional side-view cameras, paired with precise screen-space gaze targets collected under controlled fixation conditions. Each sample contains multi-view face observations together with structured facial region crops, enabling multimodal learning from both global and local visual cues. Unlike existing single-view gaze datasets, AA provides multi-view coverage from both screen-mounted and side-mounted perspectives, enabling more robust modeling under viewpoint variation and occlusion. The dataset includes subject-independent evaluation splits and a standardized data processing pipeline to support reproducible research in gaze estimation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-view multimodal dataset for screen-based gaze estimation, enabling robust gaze-based HCI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多视角多模态屏幕注视估计数据集，支持鲁棒的基于目光的人机交互
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多视角多模态屏幕注视估计数据集，支持鲁棒的基于目光的人机交互
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多视角多模态屏幕注视估计数据集，支持鲁棒的基于目光的人机交互
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents AA, a multi-view multimodal dataset for screen-based gaze estimation with eight cameras and structured facial crops, enabling robust modeling under viewpoint variation and occlusion, directly supporting gaze-based interaction research.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有注视估计数据集多为单视角，在视角变化和遮挡下鲁棒性不足。本文构建了AA数据集，包含8个屏幕摄像头和2个侧视摄像头的同步多视角面部观测，并提供结构化面部区域裁剪。数据集支持多模态学习，涵盖全局与局部线索。标准化的评估协议为基于目光的交互研究提供了重要基础设施，可直接服务于目光意图预测和人机交互应用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有注视估计数据集多为单视角，在视角变化和遮挡下鲁棒性不足。本文构建了AA数据集，包含8个屏幕摄像头和2个侧视摄像头的同步多视角面部观测，并提供结构化面部区域裁剪。数据集支持多模态学习，涵盖全局与局部线索。标准化的评估协议为基于目光的交互研究提供了重要基础设施，可直接服务于目光意图预测和人机交互应用。

quick_skim.title: Inverse reinforcement learning reveals action-oriented value signals in naturalistic decision making
quick_skim.abstract: A major challenge for cognitive neuroscience is to explain how value of a goal-directed behavior is computed in complex and naturalistic environments. Standard computational models of decision making have been highly successful in controlled, trial-based paradigms, but they are often ill-suited to real-time behavior unfolding in naturalistic paradigms. Inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) offers a way to infer latent evaluative state from observed behavior in naturalistic environments, but its neural interpretability remains largely unknown. Here, we investigated whether moment-to-moment reward trajectories derived from IRL map onto value signals in the brain during a real-time driving task performed during fMRI scanning. IRL-derived reward trajectories were most robustly associated with activity in the dorsal striatum, a region often linked to value-guided action selection. They also showed associations with distributed regions supporting additional processes, including cognitive control and sensorimotor processing. This pattern suggests that IRL reward captures distributed neural activity centered on the reward circuitry, potentially reflecting how valuation interacts with other processes. Together, these findings suggest that IRL reward provides a behaviorally grounded, temporally resolved proxy for action-oriented valuation during naturalistic decision making.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses inverse RL to infer latent evaluative state from real-time behavior
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用逆强化学习从实时行为推断潜在评估状态
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用逆强化学习从实时行为推断潜在评估状态
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用逆强化学习从实时行为推断潜在评估状态
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses inverse reinforcement learning to infer moment-to-moment reward trajectories from naturalistic driving behavior, offering a method for real-time intent recognition from behavioral data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对自然环境中实时行为建模的挑战，提出使用逆强化学习从驾驶行为中推断逐时刻奖励轨迹。研究将IRL推断的价值信号与fMRI脑信号进行映射，验证了方法的神经可解释性。该工作为从行为数据中实时识别意图提供了计算框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对自然环境中实时行为建模的挑战，提出使用逆强化学习从驾驶行为中推断逐时刻奖励轨迹。研究将IRL推断的价值信号与fMRI脑信号进行映射，验证了方法的神经可解释性。该工作为从行为数据中实时识别意图提供了计算框架。
